title: append_note input schema
description: Input contract for the append_note typed MCP tool. Authoritative source is the Zod schema in src/tools/append_note/schema.ts; this JSON Schema is the human-readable cross-reference and the form published via zodToJsonSchema at registration time. Uses the project's target_mode discriminator per ADR-003 — in specific mode, vault is required plus one of file/path; in active mode, none of vault/file/path may appear.

target_mode.description: Locator-shape discriminator per ADR-003. 'specific' = caller supplies vault + file|path. 'active' = wrapper resolves the currently-focused note via a small obsidian eval; no vault/file/path may be supplied. No additional opt-in flag is required for active mode (FR-004a — deliberate cohort exception to write_note's overwrite:true requirement, justified by append's additive-not-destructive safety profile).

vault.description: Vault display name. REQUIRED when target_mode === 'specific'; FORBIDDEN when target_mode === 'active'. Resolved via the project's lazy vault registry. Unknown vault surfaces VAULT_NOT_FOUND.

file.description: Wikilink-form note locator (bare note name, the same shape the cohort's read-side tools accept under their 'file' parameter). MUST NOT contain '[[' or ']]' brackets (FR-001a — supply the bare note name, e.g. 'My Note', not '[[My Note]]'). Alternative to 'path'; exactly one of {file, path} required when target_mode === 'specific'. Subject to the project's structural path-safety refinement (no '../', no leading '/' or '\\', no drive-letter prefix, no control characters). Layer 2 canonical-path check (post-validation) rejects symlink-escape with PATH_ESCAPES_VAULT.

path.description: Vault-relative file path. Alternative to 'file'; exactly one of {file, path} required when target_mode === 'specific'. Subject to the project's structural path-safety refinement. Brackets are NOT a special case under 'path' — vault-relative paths can in principle contain bracket characters; brackets are a wikilink-syntax artefact only.

content.description: The content to append at end-of-file. MUST be non-empty per FR-013 (empty content surfaces as VALIDATION_ERROR + details.code: CONTENT_EMPTY). No explicit max-length cap — bounded by available memory and the filesystem's max-file-size limit; for realistic notes the wrapper imposes no caller-visible ceiling. Preserved BYTE-FOR-BYTE VERBATIM per FR-010a — no trim, no normalisation, no auto-appended trailing newline. May contain line breaks; structural validity of the resulting markdown is the caller's responsibility.
examples:
- new task to track
## New section

Paragraph text.
| 2026-05-25 | shipped append_note |

inline.description: When false (default), the wrapper applies the FR-006 / FR-006a default-separator rule — if the file's existing content does NOT end with '\n' or '\r\n', a line-ending-convention-matching separator is inserted between the prior content and the new content; if the file already ends with a line break, that existing line break IS the separator and no additional one is inserted; if the file is 0 bytes, no leading separator is inserted. When true, the wrapper writes the content IMMEDIATELY AFTER the file's existing trailing byte with NO separator inserted (FR-007 — fuses onto a partial trailing line).
